name: xf_cep_not_bf_arrival2
drl: rule NE when not E0() P() then end
comment: D-149 FENCED recon -> D-150 MECHANISM CRACKED (graft arc): the bf-with-arrivals order is now EXACTLY specified by the MECHANICAL per-arrival flush simulator `tools/model_check_notorder_b.py MODEL=flush` (predict_flush), built from a BfDump graft (oracle/.../BfDump.java: listener-driven beta/executor dumps + a PropagationList reflection proxy logging every entry enqueue/execution + SegmentMemory/PathMemory linking trace). THE MACHINERY (all graft-observed, DECISIONS D-150): (1) the hidden state is the join's RIGHT-MEMORY LIST ORDER (rtm) + the staged-right-ins BACKLOG — not any per-fact stamp; (2) every op is a FIFO PropagationEntry; E0 (event) inserts FORCE-FLUSH an eval at their queue position (STREAM per-arrival flush), draining the backlog LIFO into rtm; (3) EXPIRE entries only REGISTER — ALL retracts run at QUIESCENCE (ActivationsManagerImpl.flushExpirations), AFTER every queued entry incl. post-ADV updates, in deadline order, each with its own force-eval; the last retract relinks the not and UNBLOCKS: emission = reverse(rtm) via the trg-prepend; expiry deadline = ts+@expires+1 (exact-boundary advance does NOT expire); (4) a bare-P update (empty inferred mask) is an IMMEDIATE rtm move-to-tail at its FIFO position (BetaNode.modifyObject reorder-only branch), a NO-OP if still staged, and never re-fires; (5) P staging queues the fire-loop eval ONLY while the segment is linked — an E0 right-insert processed while linked UNLINKS the unconstrained NotNode (PhreakNotNode.unlinkNotNodeOnRightInsert), which is what creates multi-epoch backlogs. VALIDATED 0-div: 9041 population scenarios (pure-bf 694 banked + 679 fresh seeds 871-873; P-first; MIXED; m2; val — the flush model SUBSUMES seg/seg2/d140 on the whole event-blocker family) + 55 probes incl. every D-149 flip-flop pair and mid-run unblock/re-block timelines. Witness stays xfail until the ENGINE PORT (Bryan gate pending); engine still runs the d140 key here. Scope: EVENT blockers only (plain-fact `not D()` = different machinery, D-140). (See also the sibling xf_cep_not_bf_arrival witness.)
